response.justification: Candidate brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience, including high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby and current Staff-level work at re:cinq involving MCP code generators and agentic workflows — directly relevant to the AI-native engineering mindset required. However, the resume lists JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and others as primary skills with no mention of Python, which is a must-have. Distributed-systems depth is present but framed more around DLang/Node than the Python ecosystem the role assumes.
strengths: Current hands-on work with MCP and agentic tooling at re:cinq aligns with the AI-native baseline
Proven distributed/high-throughput experience (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic)
Strong TDD, craftsmanship, and Staff-level scope across multiple companies
concerns: No Python listed in skills or employment history — a must-have for the role
Distributed-systems expertise is in DLang/Node rather than Python ecosystem
Limited evidence of formal stakeholder management or on-call/incident-response leadership at scale
